title: `docker-compose up` fails to find qualified image `Error response from daemon: error getting default registries to try`
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman` fails to find an image when invoked with `docker-compose` through a socket.

The `docker-compose.yaml` specifies full URL to image docker.io/d3fk/mailman2:latest

```yaml
$ cat docker-compose.yaml 
version: '3.3'
services:
    mailman2:
        container_name: mailman2
        environment:
            - URL_PATTERN=https
            - URL_ROOT=
            - SSL_FROM_CONTAINER=false
        image: docker.io/d3fk/mailman2
```

But, `docker-compose` fails with the error that it can not find short name "d3fk/mailman2:latest".

```bash
$ DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock docker-compose up
[+] Running 0/0
 ⠿ mailman2 Error                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
Error response from daemon: error getting default registries to try: short-name "d3fk/mailman2:latest" did not resolve to an alias and no unqualified-search registries are defined in "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
```

But the container exist.
```bash
$ podman search docker.io/d3fk/mailman2
INDEX      NAME                     DESCRIPTION                                      STARS   OFFICIAL  AUTOMATED
docker.io  docker.io/d3fk/mailman2  A useful compact mailman2 based on Debian bu...  5
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install `podman` and `docker-compose` from repos on Debian 11

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.15
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No.

comments.body:
This is fixed in podman 4.0 which default to docker.io for the compat api.
If you need to fix this set the `unqualified-search-registries` in registry.conf to docker.io.
@Luap99 here the qualified image name is used, and the fix is proposing to set params for unqualified images. Why you think it is the same issue?
The docker client removes the docker.io domain before sending the image name to the server. That is why we decided to always use docker.io only for the compat api starting v4
